analysis_question: Can the system identify why the VPPV loop is unreliable before unsafe continuation?
Is the policy moving correctly toward a wrong or biased visual state?
Does vertical or scale error cause near-target mismatch?
Does the target state become unreliable when the tool occludes the view?
Does the actor representation or action-progress relation reveal approach drift?
Does the final handoff fail despite repeated near-target commands?
Does the route stop unsafe continuation before recovery becomes dangerous?
Does the policy enter a failure-prone representation region?
Is the visual state estimate trustworthy?
Did the action produce the expected state transition?
Does this transition resemble known failures?
Is the rollout stuck or unstable despite continued commands?
Which perturbation family causes failure and at what severity?
Does multi-evidence mechanism routing beat single-score or uniform retry baselines?
